commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: This PR should be handled by a human maintainer because the remaining blocker is route-policy acceptance and normal draft-to-ready merge review, not a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff does not touch workflows, dependencies, install scripts, secrets, or package resolution, and it adds sensitive-media mirror safeguards. (sha=7b838cc4307c6ab04b36d15253476ca472c253d8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a gateway internal chat-surface contract, broadcasts internal message-tool source replies back to TUI chat events, preserves source-reply metadata through media/TTS normalization, and adds focused gateway/media/docs coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. for review purposes. Current main still has WebChat-only internal route handling and no TUI source-reply final broadcaster, and the PR body includes a base negative live run plus exact-head positive TUI proof.
commands.review_followup: This PR should be handled by a human maintainer because the remaining blocker is route-policy acceptance and normal draft-to-ready merge review, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(gateway): deliver internal source replies to TUI
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: TUI-originated chat runs could miss message-tool source replies because the Gateway modeled WebChat, but not TUI, as an internal chat surface.
- Solution: Move internal chat surface identity into the Gateway protocol contract, use it for route inheritance, and broadcast message-tool source replies back over Gateway chat events.
- What changed: TUI/WebChat/Control UI/native UI-mode clients are handled through a shared internal-surface contract; current-session aliases are centralized; source-reply metadata is preserved through normalization/media/TTS; transcript mirrors are upgraded with safe fallback; sensitive source media stays live-only.
- What did NOT change: External channel delivery policy, provider auth, native non-UI route inheritance, and normal Pi assistant-turn persistence.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #38957
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

TUI users should see the same visible source reply that the agent sends through the message tool. The previous route/source-surface split let the reply exist in transcript machinery, and in WebChat-oriented internal handling, while the active TUI chat stream could see no final reply.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: TUI-originated `chat.send` source replies no longer disappear from the active TUI when an agent uses the message tool for the visible source reply.
Real environment tested: Live local dev host with the managed systemd gateway stopped, no listener left on port `18789`, and a foreground gateway started from this worktree on `ws://127.0.0.1:18789` at exact pushed PR head `7b838cc4307c6ab04b36d15253476ca472c253d8` (`OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (7b838cc)`). The live run used the real terminal TUI, the configured Codex harness with `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, and the same local dev auth state. Earlier negative proof was run on exact base commit `5e0850fc548ceab82af7244938b833024e485834`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
systemctl --user stop openclaw-gateway.service
ps -ef | grep -E 'openclaw.*gateway|gateway --port|gateway run|tui' | grep -v grep || true
ss -ltnp 2>/dev/null | grep ':18789' || true
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --port 18789 --bind loopback --ws-log compact --force
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call health --json --timeout 10000
node scripts/run-node.mjs tui --session live-proof-20260520-0136 --timeout-ms 180000 --message 'OPENCLAW_EXACT_HEAD_TUI_SOURCE_REPLY_7b838cc_0136 Use the message tool to send exactly this visible reply to the current chat: TUI exact-head source reply 7b838cc 0136 visible. Do not include any other final answer text.'
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call chat.history --json --timeout 20000 --params '{"sessionKey":"live-proof-20260520-0136","limit":20}'
```
Because `tui --message` did not visibly dispatch before connect, I manually submitted the exact same prompt in the real TUI PTY. After collecting proof, I exited the TUI, stopped the foreground worktree gateway with SIGINT, and left `openclaw-gateway.service` inactive as allowed for this dev host.
Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the live exact-head TUI contained the rendered standalone reply in the active chat.
```text
TUI exact-head source reply 7b838cc 0136 visible.
```
`chat.history` for `live-proof-20260520-0136` showed session id `a06bbaa5-9077-4fa5-a034-8a6f5adf4ea0`, the user prompt nonce `OPENCLAW_EXACT_HEAD_TUI_SOURCE_REPLY_7b838cc_0136`, a `message` tool call with `{"action":"send","message":"TUI exact-head source reply 7b838cc 0136 visible."}`, and a tool result reporting `"status":"ok"`, `"deliveryStatus":"sent"`, `"channel":"webchat"`, `"target":"current-run"`, `"sourceReplyDeliveryMode":"message_tool_only"`, and `"sourceReplySink":"internal-ui"`. The durable `delivery-mirror` assistant message persisted the same text with idempotency key `bc81f146-0445-4312-bd41-5c8ba78dd0e4:internal-source-reply:0`.
Observed result after fix: The exact source reply sent through the live `message` tool appeared in the real TUI session and was also persisted as the internal source-reply mirror, proving the fixed Gateway source-reply event path reaches TUI instead of only transcript or another internal UI path.
What was not tested: Live external-channel delivery such as Telegram/Discord/Slack was not re-tested for this PR-head proof; this proof targets the reported TUI source-reply route. The explicit `tui --url ws://127.0.0.1:18789` path was not used because the URL override requires explicit credentials; the configured TUI/gateway path was used instead. The existing focused tests cover external route non-inheritance, native non-UI route inheritance, media mirrors, rich source replies, dropped managed outgoing image mirrors, and sensitive-media handling.
Before evidence: On exact base commit `5e0850fc548ceab82af7244938b833024e485834`, I started a foreground base gateway with `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --port 18789 --bind loopback --ws-log compact`, launched `node scripts/run-node.mjs tui --session live-negative-20260519-1331 --message 'Live negative proof request OPENCLAW_TUI_BASE_NEGATIVE_20260519_1331...' --timeout-ms 180000`, then manually submitted the same prompt in the real TUI PTY because base `--message` did not visibly dispatch before connect. The live base TUI returned to `connected | idle` after the run and still showed only the submitted prompt, with no standalone `OPENCLAW_TUI_BASE_NEGATIVE_20260519_1331` reply. Gateway history proved the tool did run: the model emitted `message` with `{"action":"send","message":"OPENCLAW_TUI_BASE_NEGATIVE_20260519_1331"}`, the tool result reported `"deliveryStatus":"sent"`, `"sourceReplyDeliveryMode":"message_tool_only"`, and `"sourceReplySink":"internal-ui"`, and a durable `delivery-mirror` assistant message was persisted with idempotency key `0bfe34e8-42ad-465e-a997-3a77d3dd53bc:internal-source-reply:0`. `sessions.list` reported `status=done`, `modelProvider=openai-codex`, `model=gpt-5.5`, `runtimeMs=12079`, `hasActiveRun=false`. That reproduces the reported failure shape: the source reply is sent/persisted, but the active TUI does not receive the visible reply before this patch.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Gateway route resolution and source-reply replay treated WebChat as the internal source surface but did not include the TUI client identity (`openclaw-tui`, mode `ui`).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No Gateway/TUI WebSocket test asserted that message-tool source replies are delivered to TUI clients.
- Contributing context: Source-reply metadata was lost by several normalization/media/TTS transformations, so rich/media source replies could not be reliably mirrored or replayed.

## Design Notes

- Internal chat surface identity now belongs to `src/gateway/protocol/client-info.ts`, the protocol-owned place where Gateway client ids are already defined.
- `src/utils/message-channel.ts` decides whether a client is an internal chat surface; it treats WebChat/TUI/Control UI/id-only legacy internal clients as internal, and only treats native app ids as internal when they are actually in `mode=ui`.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat-internal-source-reply.ts` now owns source-reply broadcast/mirror behavior, including transcript mirror upgrades, final-event preparation, and the fallback that strips unreadable managed outgoing image URLs when a mirror was never attached to a transcript message.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat-assistant-content.ts` owns assistant display-content projection, so `chat.ts` no longer carries the source-reply content munging inline.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` uses the shared internal-surface contract for route inheritance and source-reply broadcast, so internal chat surfaces do not accidentally inherit external delivery routes while native `mode=node` callers still can.
- Transcript mirror rewrite failures now fall back instead of being counted as successful upgrades, preventing stale or unsafe mirror content from being treated as repaired.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Seam / integration test
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, and `src/utils/message-channel.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a TUI WebSocket client receives a final chat event for a message-tool internal source reply, including route inheritance boundaries, media, delayed mirrors, and sensitive-media mirror handling.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug sits at the Gateway/client event boundary and source-surface contract, not inside an external provider.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

TUI users now see message-tool source replies in the active chat stream. Sensitive media in source replies remains live-only and is not prewritten into durable transcript mirrors.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
TUI chat.send -> agent message tool source reply -> durable mirror only -> no TUI chat final event

After:
TUI chat.send -> agent message tool source reply -> Gateway source-reply final event -> TUI sees reply
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local checkout
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.2 via repository wrappers
- Model/provider: live `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` for the terminal TUI proof; mocked agent dispatch for focused regression tests
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway TUI/WebSocket client path
- Relevant config (redacted): default local dev config; secrets were not printed

### Steps

1. Applied the TUI source-reply regression test to `origin/main`; it failed with `missing TUI source reply; seen=[]`.
2. Ran the live negative control on exact base commit `5e0850fc548ceab82af7244938b833024e485834`; the TUI prompt submitted and the model used the message tool, but the active TUI did not render the standalone source reply.
3. Ran the same focused test after this patch; it passed and observed a TUI final event for the internal source reply.
4. Stopped the existing managed gateway, confirmed no listener remained on port `18789`, started a foreground PR-head gateway from this worktree at exact head `7b838cc4307c6ab04b36d15253476ca472c253d8`, and launched a real terminal TUI with `--session live-proof-20260520-0136`.
5. Manually submitted the same proof prompt in the real TUI PTY because `--message` did not visibly dispatch before connect.
6. Verified the TUI rendered `TUI exact-head source reply 7b838cc 0136 visible.` after the live agent used the `message` tool.
7. Queried `chat.history` for both base and PR-head runs to confirm the live tool calls, internal source sink results, delivery mirrors, model/provider, and completed run state.
8. Exited the TUI, stopped the foreground worktree gateway, and left the managed gateway inactive as allowed for this dev host.
9. Ran the focused dispatch/Gateway/media/refactor checks listed below.

### Expected

TUI receives a visible final chat event for the message-tool internal source reply, while durable transcript mirrors avoid raw `MEDIA:` paths and sensitive data URLs.

### Actual

The live base TUI did not render `OPENCLAW_TUI_BASE_NEGATIVE_20260519_1331` even though history showed the `message` tool sent that same text with `sourceReplySink=internal-ui` and persisted a delivery mirror. The live exact-head TUI rendered `TUI exact-head source reply 7b838cc 0136 visible.`; history showed the `message` tool sent that same text with `sourceReplyDeliveryMode=message_tool_only` and `sourceReplySink=internal-ui`; and the regression tests passed for the Gateway/TUI source-reply event path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing live before + passing live after
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: live base failure where message-tool source reply was sent/persisted but not rendered in the active TUI, live terminal TUI source-reply delivery on exact pushed PR head `7b838cc4307c6ab04b36d15253476ca472c253d8`, live Codex harness provider invocation through `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, message-tool internal source sink delivery, TUI source-reply Gateway event delivery, source-reply media mirror upgrade, sensitive source media mirror suppression, metadata preservation through normalization/media/TTS, native non-UI route inheritance, and route non-inheritance for TUI/WebChat/Control UI source surfaces.
- Edge cases checked: image-only source replies, interactive/rich source replies, delayed source mirrors, dropped media, TTS synthesis, staged audio trust, and sensitive data-image replies.
- What you did **not** verify: live external messaging channel delivery such as Telegram/Discord/Slack.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: The source-reply path now creates an additional Gateway final event for internal UI surfaces.
  - Mitigation: Uses distinct source-reply run ids and tests ensure normal external route behavior is unchanged.
- Risk: Internal surface classification could accidentally block route inheritance for native non-UI callers.
  - Mitigation: Native app ids are internal only in `mode=ui`; focused tests cover native `mode=node` inheriting the source route.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "delivers message-tool source replies to TUI clients over gateway chat events"`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/utils/message-channel.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-resolution.test.ts` (final refactor follow-up, 5 files / 225 tests passed in 17.87s)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-assistant-content.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-internal-source-reply.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-assistant-content.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-internal-source-reply.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile /tmp/openclaw-test-src-internal-chat-refactor-final2.tsbuildinfo --pretty false`
- `codex review --base origin/main` (clean after the final refactor: no actionable correctness issues found)
- Live exact-head proof on pushed head `7b838cc4307c6ab04b36d15253476ca472c253d8` with foreground worktree gateway + real terminal TUI session `live-proof-20260520-0136`; TUI rendered `TUI exact-head source reply 7b838cc 0136 visible.` and `chat.history` showed `sourceReplyDeliveryMode=message_tool_only` / `sourceReplySink=internal-ui`.
target.author: rubencu

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
